seoTitle: Long Beach vs Long Jetty — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Long Beach and Long Jetty. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Long Beach vs Long Jetty: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Long Beach and Long Jetty.
heading: Long Beach vs Long Jetty — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Long Beach, New South Wales and Long Jetty, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Long Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Long Jetty (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Long Beach or Long Jetty safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Long Beach has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Long Jetty scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Long Beach scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Long Jetty scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Long Beach or Long Jetty?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Long Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
